content:
<@633215996508766229> Some of my best experiences playing mmos (and some gachas) were raids, and a large part of it, to me, was a sense of community and achievement – a bunch of players came together to do an event and got rewarded for doing it.

This Morimens anniversary raid on the other hand, seemed by design to give most players a sense of fomo and unjustness instead:

1. The restrictions favour established accounts, this makes new players feel left out; now if the purpose of such events is to reward veteran players, that is fine, but why does it have to be so blatantly at the expense of newer players? People register such event rewards as a loss rather than a gain, and have an outsized negative reaction to it; 

2. Time zone and kill count limits participation; Again, this has been announced as a public event for all players, yet if people in Europe choose to sleep, since maintenance is after midnight, the chance to take part will be gone. That is such a terrible design choice that excludes a certain portion of players, after making them register this as something they could have done and giving them a sense of losing out.

So this makes me wonder what the goal of such event is? 

a) If the goal is participation and community building, then make the rewards and opportunity equal to all, so everyone can have a grand old time and whales/veterans can still show off their support units and rankings

b) If the goal is to reward veteran players, then instead of a public event, say, rewards are given out as a login-for-a-year bonus, or like the paid tier achievements as something that unlocks after a certain amount has been spent on the game, it would achieve very similar reward distribution while not dangling in others faces by disguising it as an something that they could have done
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Unfair Raid Event

content:
I thought of a fun solution to some issues - add the 'beating the dead horse' mode to the boss.
The idea is this - there are still 50k attempts per boss. But when they expire the boss enters a 'dead horse' state - 'hp is reduced to 1 and all revives are removed'. At this point people can still enter the raid and kill it with literally 1 attack of anything and still get the clear reward. They will not be counted for leaderboard for balance.
All bosses will remain open for this for a week after the last one dies. That way anyone who is late can get all the rewards with less trouble and without time limitations.

thread_title: Raid Problems and Solutions (The feedbacks have been shared with Light )

content:
Basically it's a rogulike mode in which you start on floor 0/hub where you choose the story you will play (each story is based on a different major character), and then you explore I think 8? floors (that is kinda similar to morimens floor design - enemies, encounters,shop and floor boss).
Each story have universal relics which are the same for all stories, and unique one's, same with encounters and shop relics.
After you complete the run you will get unique currency that you can use to upgrade the "talents tree" of the mode.
Big part for me was that we could make run more difficult by giving enemies different buffs& global debuffs for yourself, and also there were different achievements,and some of them are really grindy - so it adds up a lot of replayability.
As you see there's a lot of similarities between this mode and morimens mechanics, and I would love to someday get something similar, but I understand that creating something as big as this is very hard, and will just shut up for now cause this message is already far too big.

content:
I resolved it, but I'll leave the info in case it helps narrow down any issues.
Going off the weird d3d11 errors, I gave disabling GPU drivers a shot and attempting to open the game using only CPU graphics. This worked, and then that seemingly broke it out of this loop, because after reboot it now works with my GPU drivers as well. My guess is that for some reason it was attempting to use the drivers relating to my old GPU despite them being uninstalled with DDU? I'm really unsure, but it is fixed at least for me. 

CPU is AMD Ryzen 7 5700G, GPU is RTX 5060TI, and the old GPU was a RTX 2070.

Character interface display: Restore 21 health points plus an additional 5 HP for every discarded card;

Final HP recovery= Basic HP recovery amount + additional HP recovery amount for each discarded card
